About: It is a series of cooperative board games inspired by escape rooms.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with escape rooms.
ConsistsOf: It consists of numbered cards depicting locations, objects, or characters.
CreatedBy: It was created by Cyril Demaegd.
CreatedTo: It was created to recreate the adrenaline rush and tension experienced during the final minutes of a live escape room within a card game format.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Cyril Demaegd.
HasA: It has a mandatory smartphone application.
It has specific accessories for each scenario.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by cooperative gameplay.
It is characterized by scenarios designed to last from 60 minutes to 90 minutes each.
It is characterized by the resolution of riddles.
It is characterized by the exploration of an imaginary world.
It is characterized by numbered cards depicting locations, objects, or characters.
It is characterized by riddles or combinations of multiple cards allowing players to obtain the number of the next card.
It is characterized by a mandatory smartphone application.
It is characterized by the application serving as a timer.
It is characterized by the application calculating the final score.
It is characterized by scenarios that are reusable by other players afterward.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by real-life escape rooms.
It was influenced by "Fox in a Box" in Paris.
IsA: It is a cooperative board game series.
It is a board game series.
It is a young kids version.
KnownAs: It is known under the working title "Project 59:59" during development.
KnownFor: It is known for its escape room inspiration.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Space Cowboys.
PublishedAs: It was published as numerous box sets.
PublishedBy: It was published by Space Cowboys.
UsedFor: It is used for solving riddles.
It is used for exploring an imaginary world.
It is used for entering codes.
It is used for operating mechanisms.
It is used for listening to audio clues.
It is used for obtaining hints.
It is used for calculating the final score.
UsedTo: It is used to solve a scenario proposed by the game within a time limit.
WrittenBy: It was written by Cyril Demaegd.
It was written by Alice Carroll.
It was written by Thomas Cauet.
It was written by Fabrice Mazza.
It was written by Arnaud Ladagnous.
It was written by Sébastien Pauchon.
It was written by Billy Stevenson.
It was written by Arch Stanton.
It was written by Lewis Cheshire.
It was written by Germain Winzenschtark.
It was written by Vincent Goyat.
It was written by Dave Neale.
It was written by Mathieu Casnin.
It was written by Guillaume Montiage.
It was written by Aristide Bruyant.
It was written by Yohan Servais.
It was written by Luna Marie.
It was written by Guilaine Didiern.
It was written by Gabriel Dumerin.
It was written by Théo Rivière.
It was written by Jérémy Fraile.
It was written by Jeremy Koch.
It was written by Marion du Faouët.
It was written by Renaud Chaillat.
It was written by Jack Gitz.
It was written by Lewis Trondheim.
It was written by Pierre Chabosy.
It was written by Julien Jovis.
It was written by Romain Pujol.
